activities_projects.title: Sector Classification with Justification
activities_projects.objective: To classify occupations into sectors and justify the reasoning.
steps: List 15 occupations from daily life.
Classify them into primary, secondary, and tertiary sectors.
Write one reason for each classification.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand sector classification with logical reasoning.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check accuracy of classification and clarity of justification.

activities_projects.title: Workforce Distribution Analysis
activities_projects.objective: To analyze employment distribution among sectors.
steps: Collect workforce percentage data from the textbook.
Draw a bar graph showing employment in each sector.
Write two conclusions based on the graph.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret employment data and identify trends.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess graph accuracy and quality of conclusions.

activities_projects.title: GDP vs Employment Comparison
activities_projects.objective: To compare GDP contribution and employment share of sectors.
steps: Prepare a table comparing GDP contribution and employment percentage.
Analyze differences between the two.
Write a short explanation for the mismatch.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand imbalance between employment and GDP contribution.
activities_projects.evaluation: Check correctness of table and analytical explanation.

activities_projects.title: Public vs Private Sector Case Study
activities_projects.objective: To examine ownership and objectives of public and private sectors.
steps: Select one public sector and one private sector company.
Compare their objectives and services.
Present findings in a short report.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students differentiate public and private sector roles.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate clarity of comparison and understanding.

activities_projects.title: Organized vs Unorganized Sector Survey
activities_projects.objective: To analyze working conditions in different sectors.
steps: Survey five workers (real or through discussion).
Identify whether they belong to organized or unorganized sector.
Compare wages, job security, and benefits.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand differences in working conditions.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess survey completeness and comparison quality.

activities_projects.title: Interdependence of Sectors Flowchart
activities_projects.objective: To understand how sectors depend on each other.
steps: Identify one example from each sector (farmer, factory, shop).
Draw a flowchart showing how they are connected.
Explain the interdependence in 4–5 lines.

activities_projects.title: Service Sector Growth Study
activities_projects.objective: To analyze reasons for growth of the tertiary sector.
steps: List reasons for the growth of the service sector.
Provide real-life examples (banking, IT, transport).
Write a short paragraph explaining its importance.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand factors behind service sector expansion.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess relevance of reasons and explanation.

activities_projects.title: Sector-wise Income Comparison
activities_projects.objective: To compare income levels across sectors.
steps: Collect sample monthly income data from three sectors.
Calculate annual income for each.
Compare and state two observations.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze income differences among sectors.

activities_projects.title: Impact of Technology on Sectors
activities_projects.objective: To study how technology affects different sectors.
steps: Identify one example of technology use in each sector.
Explain how it improves productivity.
Write a brief summary.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand role of technology in economic growth.
activities_projects.evaluation: Evaluate clarity of examples and explanation.

activities_projects.title: Local Economic Sector Mapping
activities_projects.objective: To identify and map sector activities in the locality.
steps: Draw a simple map of your locality.
Mark at least two activities from each sector.
Write two observations about sector dominance.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect theoretical knowledge with real-life surroundings.
activities_projects.evaluation: Assess map clarity and relevance of observations.
